title: pholisma sonorae
intro: Pholisma sonorae, also known as sand plant or beach morning glory, is a perennial succulent plant that grows in coastal areas of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, forming dense mats of stems and leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat wounds, burns, and other skin irritations. Pholisma sonorae has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Pholisma sonorae typically involve drying and crushing the plant's roots and stems, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves for topical application.
